scenario_a.description: HC win(TATA) → COA sets aside award
HC sets aside → COA restores award
HC sets aside → COA upholds setting aside

decomposition.label: Total Statement of Claim
decomposition.note: Sum across 1 DFCCIL claims in portfolio

decomposition.label: Arbitration win probability
decomposition.note: Expert judgment based on DFCCIL historical outcomes; 80% probability TATA wins first arbitration

decomposition.label: Expected quantum given win
decomposition.note: 5-band quantum distribution: 70% chance of 80-100% SOC, 15% chance of 0-20% SOC, remainder spread across middle bands

decomposition.label: Court survival given arb win
decomposition.note: Empirical from MC: probability award survives post-arb court challenges (Domestic: S.34→S.37→SLP; SIAC: HC→COA). Based on 89 arb-won paths.

decomposition.label: Recovery via re-arbitration path
decomposition.note: Additional value from RESTART paths: arb_lose → court reverses adverse award → fresh re-arbitration (P=80%) → survive courts. Adds ₹710.1 Cr to expected recovery.

decomposition.label: Expected amount collected
decomposition.note: Total expected collection = direct path (₹645.1 Cr) + RESTART contribution (₹710.1 Cr). MC average over 103 paths.

decomposition.label: Minus expected legal costs
decomposition.note: All-stage legal costs: tribunal fees, counsel, DAB, arbitration, court challenges. Average ₹23.2 Cr per claim.
